description: Security audit findings from 2026-03-07. Address path traversal, race conditions, file permissions, and input validation issues identified during codebase review.
title: Security Hardening

description:
Content names from jolene.toml (e.g. commands = ["../../../etc/passwd"]) are joined into paths without explicit path component validation. While Rust's Path::join() is safe against absolute path injection, ../ traversal within the joined path could escape intended directories.

**Location:** src/validation.rs:93-128

**Fix:** Validate that content names contain no /, \, or .. components before using them in path operations. Reject any name that is not a simple filename component.
title: Validate manifest content names against path traversal

description:
No lock file exists to prevent concurrent jolene processes from corrupting state. Two simultaneous 'jolene install' commands could both load old state, execute symlinks, and the last writer wins — losing the first writer's state entries. The atomic write (temp file + rename) prevents file corruption but not logical races.

**Location:** src/state.rs

**Fix:** Implement advisory file locking using a lock file (~/.jolene/.lock) acquired before loading state and released after writing. Use flock/fcntl-style locking for cross-process safety.
title: Add advisory file locking for concurrent access

description:
Between create_dir_all() and symlink() in symlink creation, an attacker could replace the parent directory with a symlink to an attacker-controlled location. The symlink would then be created inside the wrong directory.

**Location:** src/symlink.rs:137-140

**Fix:** After create_dir_all(), re-canonicalize the parent path and verify it still resolves to the expected location before creating the symlink. Alternatively, open the parent directory with O_NOFOLLOW semantics and create the symlink relative to the directory fd.
title: Mitigate symlink TOCTOU race in directory creation

description:
state.json is created via NamedTempFile with default umask permissions, potentially world-readable. It contains clone URLs and filesystem paths that could leak information about the user's setup.

**Location:** src/state.rs:51

**Fix:** After creating the temp file and before renaming, set file permissions to 0o600 (owner read/write only) using std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt.
title: Set explicit permissions on state file

description:
Git URLs from --url are passed directly to git clone without format validation. While Command::new("git").args(...) doesn't invoke a shell (no shell injection), malformed URLs or URLs with embedded credentials are accepted without validation.

**Location:** src/git.rs:12-23

**Fix:** Validate URL scheme — allow https://, git://, ssh:// and reject obviously malformed inputs. Consider warning on URLs containing embedded credentials (user:pass@host).
title: Validate git URL scheme before cloning

description:
The codebase has several race condition windows (state file TOCTOU, symlink creation TOCTOU, doctor command symlink read). These should be documented so users understand the concurrency model.

**Fix:** Add a section to README or SPEC documenting:
- Jolene is not safe to run concurrently (until file locking is added)
- Known race windows and their impact
- Recommendation to avoid parallel invocations
title: Document concurrency limitations
